findings.title: Re-sync reclassifies format for all synced items
findings.why_it_matters: bulkUpsertAbsBooks replaces format and source on every re-sync. If ABS metadata changes between syncs (e.g., narrators removed due to ABS bug or metadata edit), an audiobook could be reclassified as 'epub' on re-sync. The plan acknowledges this for audiobooks without narrator metadata, but any ABS metadata change affecting the heuristic triggers reclassification.
evidence: bulkUpsertAbsBooks merge semantics (from plan: 'replaces format and source from newly-mapped books on re-sync')
The heuristic is re-evaluated on every sync from ABS metadata
Plan's own R6 tension documentation acknowledges this risk
findings.suggested_fix: Consider persisting the detected format separately from the sync-derived format, so re-sync only updates when confidence is high (e.g., ABS says 'ebook' but local says 'audiobook' — trust local unless ABS metadata is unambiguous)

residual_risks: Sync loop filter widening (line 334) now accepts 'ebook' items. If a future ABS version returns a new mediaType that should also be accepted (e.g., 'comic'), the filter must be updated independently.
testing_gaps: No E2E test validates the full sync-and-read chain for ebooks from ABS
